title: Sequenced Cell Episode
objective: Build a two-voice episode by sequencing one two-note cell down a step per beat over the given D-minor lament bass, keeping the voices directionally independent.
prompt: Replace rests in V0 with parser-valid half-beat notes (duration 0.5) forming ONE two-note cell per beat, the same cell shape sequenced down one diatonic step per beat for at least four consecutive statements starting at beat 0 (verify: any two statements match at the diatonic layer), then a free approach into the cadence with a final note of duration 1 at beat 7. Integer-beat tones must be consonant against the bass. The bass descends by step; do not lock V0's rhythm and direction to it - the sequence supplies repetition, the offset supplies harmony, and internal direction changes (a cell whose two tones move opposite to the statement-to-statement descent) supply independence.
success_criteria: Every row remains strict parser-valid MidGrid.
V1 bass remains unchanged, including hold cells.
V0 states one cell shape per beat, sequenced down a step per beat for at least four statements from beat 0.
No parallel perfects and no sustained same-direction co-movement with the bass.
Corrected solution has no evaluator errors.

interval_rules.label: Integer-beat cell boundaries must be consonant
